pwa.installText: Install fitlo on your device for faster access and a real app-like experience.
pwa.howToInstall: How to install on this device:
pwa.manualIos: In Safari, tap the Share button (the square with an up arrow), then choose “Add to Home Screen”.
pwa.manualAndroid: In Chrome, tap the three-dot menu (⋮) at the top, then choose “Add to Home screen” or “Install app”.
pwa.manualDesktop: Click the install icon in your browser's address bar, or choose “Install fitlo” from the browser menu.

footer.tagline: The bilingual coaching & training-program platform — for coaches and their athletes.

about.title: About fitlo
about.intro: fitlo is a tool for fitness coaches who want to write professional training programs and support their athletes — without scattered files and lost DMs.
about.mission: Most coaches lose hours to repetitive work: rebuilding the same programs, sending files over chat, and chasing client details. fitlo brings it into one place so your attention stays on what actually matters — training quality and client progress.
about.value1: Persian and RTL from the ground up — not a half-translated foreign tool.
about.value2: Write a program against a phone number; the student doesn't need an account yet.
about.value3: Your own exercise library with GIFs and video links, plus a clean PDF export.
about.value4: A public page for every coach, collecting student requests directly.

forms.hasErrors: Please fill in the required fields

landing.metaTitle: fitlo — Online Coaching & Training Program App for Fitness
landing.metaDescription: fitlo is the app for online fitness and bodybuilding coaches: write day-by-day training programs, manage bodybuilding exercises with GIFs and video, and track your students' online training.
landing.keywords: training program, online coach, online training, bodybuilding workouts, fitness, online personal trainer, coaching software, workout planner
landing.title: Write and track training programs, together
landing.subtitle: Coaches write day-by-day training programs and send them straight to their students; students see their program on their phone the moment it's ready. Start free, with just a phone number.
landing.subtitleRich: Coaches write day-by-day training programs and send them straight to their students; students see their program on their phone the moment it's ready. Start free, with just a phone number.
landing.ctaHint: Sign in or sign up free — all you need is your phone number.

features.programs: Day-by-day training programs, with superset & triset support
features.library: Your own exercise library — GIFs, video links, and notes

coaches.title: Coaches on fitlo
coaches.subtitle: Trainers writing programs on fitlo. Tap a card to see their page and request a program.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.subtitle: Plans are based on how many students you coach. Start on the free plan; it's always free for students.
pricing.trialDesc: Start free now and write a program for your first student. Upgrade whenever you're ready.
pricing.trialNote: Higher plan prices will be announced soon. Until then, start on the free plan.

landing.howSubtitle: Whether you coach or train, get going in three simple steps.

coachSteps.heading: For coaches
coachSteps.s1Text: Add your exercises once, then write day-by-day programs with supersets and export a clean PDF.
coachSteps.s2Text: The student doesn't need an account yet — programs link automatically when they sign up with that number.
coachSteps.s3Text: Post your link-in-bio and collect student requests straight in your inbox.

studentSteps.heading: For students
studentSteps.s1Text: Open the coach's public page, fill in your details, and send a program request.
studentSteps.s2Text: Track your request status in your panel; if it's declined, you'll see the reason.
studentSteps.s3Text: Programs appear automatically once published, and you can download them as a PDF.

faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
faq.q1: Do I need to install anything?
faq.a1: No. fitlo is a web app that runs in your browser — you can also add it to your phone's home screen.
faq.a2: With your phone number. The first time you verify by SMS code and choose a password; after that you sign in with just that password (or a one-time code whenever you prefer).
faq.a3: Coaches start on the free plan (up to 1 student, always free). Upgrade for more: Economy (up to 10 students), Standard (up to 25 students), or Professional (unlimited students). You can write and edit multiple programs per student. Plan pricing is coming soon. It's always free for students.
faq.q4: What if a coach wrote a program before I signed up?
faq.a4: Just register with the same number the coach entered — every program links to your account automatically.

onboarding.title: Getting started
onboarding.step1Text: Add a photo and bio so you look professional to students.
onboarding.step2Text: Build your exercise library so you can write programs fast.
onboarding.step3Text: Create and publish a day-by-day program for a student.
onboarding.step4Text: Add your card number and price so students see them on the request form.

studentHome.help1: Programs your coaches write for you appear here automatically.
studentHome.help2: To get a new program, open a coach's public link and send a request.
studentHome.help3: Track your request status under "My requests".

coachHelp.title: Coach help center
coachHelp.subtitle: How to do everything, step by step. Tap a topic to expand it.

addExercise.title: Add an exercise and its GIF
addExercise.intro: Build your exercise library first; then reuse those movements across every program.
steps: Go to "Exercises" and tap "New exercise".
Enter the exercise name and its default sets and reps.
Upload a GIF to show the movement (a GIF or image file).
Optional: add a demo video link (YouTube/Aparat) and a form note.
Save — the exercise joins your library, ready to use in programs.

categories.title: Organize exercises into categories
categories.intro: Categories like Chest, Legs and Back keep your library tidy and quick to search.
steps: In "Exercises", tap "Manage categories".
Type a category name and add it, e.g. "Chest".
When creating or editing an exercise, pick its category.
You can now filter the exercise list by category.

buildProgram.title: Build a program from your exercises (and search them)
buildProgram.intro: You build the program day by day from the very exercises you uploaded.
steps: Go to "Programs" and tap "New program".
Enter the student's phone and a program name — they don't need an account yet.
Set the days per week, then tap "Add exercise" for each day.
In the picker, find the exercise by search or category filter, and add it.
Adjust sets/reps per exercise and drag to reorder them.
Tap "Save draft" or "Publish" — once published the student can see it.

supersets.title: Create a superset or triset
supersets.intro: A superset is two moves back-to-back with no rest. A triset is the same with three.
steps: Add an exercise to the day.
Under that exercise, tap "Superset" (or "Add to superset").
Pick the second exercise — the two now show as one superset group.
For a triset, add one more exercise to the same group.
Superset moves are boxed together so the student understands the order.

templates.title: What ready-made programs are for
templates.intro: Build your common programs once and assign them to many students — without rewriting.
steps: Go to "Ready-made programs" and create a new one, e.g. "Beginner plan".
Lay out the days and exercises like a normal program, and save.
Whenever you want, tap "Assign" and enter a student's phone to spin up a real program for them.
You can edit, delete or search templates by name.

studentView.title: How the student sees the programs
studentView.intro: After you publish, the student signs in with their phone and sees their program.
steps: Publish the program (not draft).
The student registers or logs in with the same number you wrote it for.
Every program you wrote for them links to their account automatically.
They see it day by day with exercise GIFs and sets/reps, and can download a PDF.

publicLink.title: What the public coach link is
publicLink.intro: A public page at /c/<handle> that you paste into your social-media bio.
steps: Go to "Profile" and choose a link handle (lowercase Latin letters).
Save the profile — your public link is now live.
Copy the link and put it in your Instagram or Telegram bio.
Anyone who taps it sees your profile and can send a program request.

requestForm.title: The request form and student info
requestForm.intro: New students send requests from your public page; their details land straight in your inbox.
steps: From your public link, the student fills the request form: height, weight, history, photos and a payment receipt.
Requests appear for you under "Requests".
You can view the details and photos and accept or decline the request.
"Start program" opens the builder pre-filled with that student's info.

cardNumber.title: Why put a card number in your profile
cardNumber.intro: To collect payment from students by card transfer, set your card number and price in the student form.
steps: Go to "Student form" and enter your card number, cardholder name and program price.
This is shown to the student in the request form on your public page.
The student transfers the amount and uploads the receipt in that same form.
You see the receipt under "Requests", then start their program.

billing.title: Subscription
billing.subtitle: Your plan is based on how many students you coach; write and edit as many programs per student as you like.
billing.plansSubtitle: Plans are based on how many students you coach. Pricing will be announced soon.
billing.simulateNote: Test mode: no real gateway configured — payment is simulated.
billing.bannerExpired: Your plan's write access has ended. Renew to keep creating or editing (your existing data stays viewable).
billing.lockedTitle: An active plan is required to create or edit — for now you can only view.

publicPage.what: This is the page students see — your name, photo, bio and contact links — and where they send you a program request.

publicPage.unsaved: Your new handle isn't saved yet — until you save, the link above still points to the old one.
publicPage.tip: Put the link in your Instagram bio, story highlights and pinned post so requests land straight in your inbox.

categories.what: Categories exist purely to organize your own exercises (e.g. Chest, Legs). You use them to filter quickly while writing a program — students never see them.
